channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Putin Arrives in Beijing for Summit With Xi Following Trump Visit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin landed in China for a two-day state visit to meet with Xi Jinping, just days after Trump's high-profile Beijing summit, as both leaders seek to project stability amid global tensions.

item.title: Chinese Military Secretly Trained Russian Forces for Ukraine War
item.description: China's armed forces covertly trained around 200 Russian military personnel late last year, with those soldiers now returning to fight in Ukraine, according to European intelligence agencies and documents seen by Reuters.

item.title: US Senate Advances Bill to End Iran War After Primary Defeat Flips Vote
item.description: The Senate advanced legislation to force Trump to withdraw from the Iran war, as Senator Cassidy flipped his vote following his primary loss, signaling growing congressional resistance to the conflict.

item.title: Federal Reserve to Hold Rates Steady as War-Driven Inflation Persists
item.description: Most economists polled by Reuters expect the Fed to avoid cutting interest rates this year, pushing rate reduction expectations into 2026 as the Iran conflict continues to pressure global energy prices and inflation.

item.title: Trump Approval Drops to 35% Amid Softening Republican Support
item.description: President Trump's approval rating fell to 35% in a Reuters/Ipsos poll, just above the 34% low of his presidency seen last month, as Republican support continues to soften.

item.title: Pentagon Cuts US Brigade Presence in Europe, Delays Poland Deployment
item.description: The Pentagon has reduced the US brigade presence in Europe and delayed planned deployments to Poland amid shifting priorities and budget constraints.

item.title: US Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The Senate has advanced a resolution aimed at limiting President Trump's authority to conduct military operations against Iran without congressional approval.

item.title: Iran Rejects US School Strike Claim as 'Baseless Fabrication'
item.description: Iran has dismissed US allegations that it targeted a school as a "baseless fabrication," escalating the war of narratives between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: US House Foreign Affairs Chair Warns AI Sales to China Could Make America 'Loser'
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee chairman warned that selling advanced AI technology to China could undermine US technological dominance and strategic interests.

item.title: Trump's Iran-Israel War Strategy Tests Gulf State Alliances
item.description: President Trump's approach to the Iran conflict is putting unprecedented strain on Gulf state partnerships as the Hormuz crisis deepens.

item.title: Ukraine's Evolving Strategy in the Russia War
item.description: An analysis of how Ukraine's military doctrine has adapted as the conflict with Russia enters a new phase with shifting Western support.

item.title: Somaliland and Israel Announce Mutual Embassies in Jerusalem and Hargeisa
item.description: Somaliland and Israel have established formal diplomatic relations with mutual embassy announcements, marking a significant shift in regional diplomacy.

item.title: US Charges 7 Chinese Executives and 4 Shipping Firms Linked to Container Cartel
item.description: The US Justice Department has charged multiple Chinese executives and shipping companies over alleged container shipping cartel operations.

item.title: Iran Warns It Could Open New Fronts After US Threatens Large-Scale Assault
item.description: Iran has threatened to escalate the conflict by opening new fronts in response to US warnings of a large-scale military operation.

item.title: More Than 16,000 People Killed in Russia's All-Out War on Ukraine: UN
item.description: The United Nations has documented over 16,000 civilian deaths in Ukraine since Russia's full-scale invasion began.

item.title: US Crude Inventories Fall Quickly But Still Up This Year
item.description: US crude stockpiles have seen sharp draws recently but remain elevated compared to start-of-year levels as global energy markets adjust to Iran conflict disruptions.

item.title: Ukraine Drone Shot Down Near Estonia's Border With Russia
item.description: Estonian authorities reported that a Ukrainian drone was intercepted near the Russian border, raising concerns about conflict spillover into NATO territory.

item.title: US Plans to Shrink Forces Available for NATO During Crises
item.description: The US is planning to reduce the number of forces it makes available to NATO during crisis situations, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Standard Chartered to Cut Over 7,000 Jobs in AI Efficiency Push
item.description: Standard Chartered announced plans to eliminate more than 7,000 positions as part of an AI-driven efficiency initiative aimed at replacing lower-value human roles.

item.title: Rubio to Attend NATO Talks, Pay First Visit to India
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will attend NATO meetings and make his first official visit to India as the administration seeks to strengthen Indo-Pacific partnerships.

item.title: BBC News Coverage
item.description: BBC News continues to provide coverage of ongoing global developments.

item.title: At Least 22 Killed in Israeli Attacks on Lebanon in 24 Hours
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry reports at least 22 people killed in Israeli attacks over the past day as cross-border violence intensifies.

item.title: Russia's Loudest Ukraine Hardliner 'No Longer Matters' in Peace Talks
item.description: Officials say Russia's most vocal Ukraine war advocate has lost influence in ongoing peace negotiations.

item.title: UK Quietly Issues Sanctions Waivers on Russian Oil Products
item.description: The UK has reportedly issued quiet waivers allowing continued trade in certain Russian oil products despite official sanctions.

item.title: Chinese Aircraft Carrier Kicks Off Drills in Western Pacific
item.description: China's aircraft carrier group has begun military exercises in the Western Pacific amid rising tensions with Japan over territorial disputes.
